howItWorks.intro: This paint mixing calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Ratios auto-normalize if they don't sum to 100%
Always mix slightly more than needed — color matching is difficult
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How do I mix custom paint colors?
faq.answer: Start with the lightest color as your base. Add darker colors in small amounts — it takes much less dark to shift a light color than vice versa. Mix thoroughly between additions. Keep notes of ratios for reproducibility.

faq.question: Can I mix different brands of paint?
faq.answer: Same type only. Latex with latex, acrylic with acrylic, oil with oil. Mixing different types can cause curdling, poor adhesion, or uneven drying. Within the same type, different brands generally mix fine.

faq.question: How do I match an existing color?
faq.answer: Most paint stores offer color matching from a sample or photo. For DIY, mix in small test batches, apply to cardboard, and let it dry — paint dries darker. Keep precise ratio notes once you achieve the right color.

sections.title: What Is the Paint Mixing Calculator?
sections.body: The Paint Mixing Calculator is a free online tool designed for crafters and makers who need quick, accurate calculations in the crafting and DIY space. By entering your total amount needed, color 1 percentage, color 2 percentage, you get instant results including color 1 amount, color 2 amount, color 3 amount. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting color 1 amount right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In crafting and DIY, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by crafters and makers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to plan your projects with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your total amount needed and need to find the right color 1 amount. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
